title: 9 Herbal Essential Oils For Over Bleeding During Periods
intro_desc: If you experience heavy bleeding during your period, herbal essential oils can be a helpful remedy. Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender oil, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce bleeding. When inhaled, it can also calm your mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate heavy bleeding.  Angelica archangelica, or angelica root oil, is another herb that can help alleviate heavy bleeding. It has a compound called ligustilide, which can help reduce menstrual flow by relaxing the uterine muscles. This can also help reduce cramps and discomfort associated with heavy bleeding.  Vitex agnus-castus, or chasteberry oil, is a natural hormone balancer that can help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce heavy bleeding. It contains a compound called agnuside, which can help reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that can stimulate uterine contractions and lead to heavy bleeding.  When you use herbal essential oils, you can experience several benefits. For one, you can reduce the amount of bleeding, making your period more manageable. You can also reduce your risk of anemia, which can occur when you lose too much blood during your period. Additionally, you can reduce your risk of infections, such as endometritis, which can occur when bacteria enter the uterus through heavy bleeding. By using herbal essential oils, you can take control of your menstrual health and reduce the discomfort and pain associated with heavy bleeding.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for over bleeding during periods

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which are the main active constituents responsible for its benefits. These compounds have a regulating effect on the body's hormonal balance, helping to reduce excessive bleeding during periods. The antispasmodic properties of linalool also help to relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramping and heavy flow. Additionally, the essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties soothe the uterine lining, promoting a healthy environment for menstrual flow. By regulating hormonal balance and reducing inflammation, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils can help alleviate heavy bleeding and related symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for over bleeding during periods
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil).
Place the flowers in a clean glass jar and pour the carrier oil over them. Store in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Add 10 drops of vitamin E oil to the liquid and mix well. Vitamin E oil helps to preserve the essential oil.
Store the Lavandula angustifolia essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Use 5-7 drops in a warm bath or mix with a carrier oil for massage during periods.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica essential oils contains bioactive constituents like angelic acid, isobutyl angelate, and ligustilide. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe and relax the uterine muscles. The antispasmodic properties of angelic acid and ligustilide help to reduce cramping and spasms, while isobutyl angelate has a calming effect on the nervous system. The essential oil's ability to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles helps to reduce heavy bleeding and alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Angelica archangelica essential oils may help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of heavy bleeding.
remedies.image_alt: Angelica archangelica herbal essential oils for over bleeding during periods
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Angelica archangelica roots and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or jojoba oil)
Clean and chop the roots into small pieces, then fill a clean glass jar with 1/2 cup of the chopped roots
Pour 1 cup of carrier oil over the chopped roots, making sure they are fully covered
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids and use the oil as needed

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus essential oils contains bioactive constituents like aucubin, rosmarinic acid, and ursolic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce heavy bleeding during periods. The essential oil's flavonoids, such as vitexin and isovitexin, have been shown to regulate hormonal balances, particularly estrogen and progesterone levels, which can contribute to excessive bleeding. Additionally, the essential oil's coumarins, like scopoletin, can help improve blood clotting and reduce menstrual flow. By regulating hormonal balances and improving blood clotting, Vitex agnus-castus essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of over bleeding during periods.
remedies.image_alt: Vitex agnus-castus herbal essential oils for over bleeding during periods
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Vitex agnus-castus flowers and 2 cups of carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil).
Combine the dried flowers and carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Stir well to mix.
Place the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This is called 'infusion'.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Bottle the essential oil and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops in a warm bath or mix with a carrier oil for topical use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils contains bioactive constituents like bilobalide and ginkgolides. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding. The flavonoids and terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba essential oils have a blood-thinning effect, which may help slow down bleeding during periods. The antioxidant properties of these compounds also help to reduce oxidative stress and promote a healthy uterine environment. By using Ginkgo biloba essential oils, women may experience relief from heavy bleeding and menstrual discomfort due to its ability to regulate blood flow and reduce inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for over bleeding during periods
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba leaves and 2 cups of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil).
Place the Ginkgo biloba leaves in a clean glass jar and cover them with carrier oil.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Discard the used Ginkgo biloba leaves and store the Ginkgo biloba essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils contains linalool, citral, and geraniol as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to ease menstrual cramps and reduce heavy bleeding during periods. Linalool, in particular, has been shown to have a positive effect on the uterine muscles, helping to regulate contractions and prevent excessive bleeding. Additionally, citral and geraniol have a calming effect on the body, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate menstrual issues. By using Melissa officinalis essential oils, women can potentially experience relief from heavy bleeding and painful periods.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal essential oils for over bleeding during periods
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis flowers and 2 cups of carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil).
Place the Melissa officinalis flowers in a clean glass jar, then pour the carrier oil over them. Stir well.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This allows the oil to infuse.
After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container. Discard the flowers.

Store the Melissa officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Use 5-7 drops in a warm bath or mix with a carrier oil for a massage oil to help ease over bleeding during periods.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa essential oils contains a compound called actein, which helps to regulate hormonal balance in the body. This balance is essential in reducing excessive bleeding during periods. The oil also contains other bioactive constituents like sesquiterpenes and triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to ease cramping and discomfort associated with heavy bleeding. The antispasmodic properties of Cimicifuga racemosa essential oils can also help to relax the uterine muscles, reducing the severity of bleeding. By regulating hormonal balance and easing cramping and discomfort, Cimicifuga racemosa essential oils may help to provide relief from over bleeding during periods.
remedies.image_alt: Cimicifuga racemosa herbal essential oils for over bleeding during periods
remedy_procedure: Gather materials: Cimicifuga racemosa roots (1 cup), Carrier oil (1 cup), A clean glass jar, Cheesecloth, A dark glass bottle.
Dry the Cimicifuga racemosa roots in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine the dried roots and carrier oil in the glass jar. Let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a bowl. Discard the solids and transfer the liquid to the dark glass bottle.
Label the bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the essential oil with a carrier oil (1 tablespoon) for relief from over bleeding.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains a compound called curcumin, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. Curcumin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and balance the uterine system, reducing heavy bleeding during periods. Curcuma longa essential oils also contains sesquiterpenes, which have a regulating effect on the menstrual cycle, helping to normalize blood flow and prevent excessive bleeding. The essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce cramping and discomfort associated with heavy menstrual bleeding. By regulating the uterine system and reducing inflammation, Curcuma longa essential oils helps to promote a balanced and healthy menstrual cycle.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for over bleeding during periods
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Curcuma longa rhizomes and 2 cups of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.
Crush 1 tablespoon of the dried Curcuma longa rhizomes into small pieces.
Combine the crushed rhizomes with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth into another container, and discard the solids. Use 5-7 drops of the essential oil in a carrier oil for external use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains a compound called gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce menstrual cramp pain and bleeding. Gingerol also has a compound called zingerone, which has a vasodilating effect, causing blood vessels to dilate and reduce blood pressure, thus reducing heavy bleeding during periods. The warming properties of gingerol also help to stimulate blood flow and improve circulation, which can help reduce heavy bleeding and promote a healthy menstrual cycle. Additionally, gingerol has a compound called shogaol, which has a uterine-stimulating effect, helping to tone the uterine muscles and regulate menstrual flow. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy blood flow, Zingiber officinale essential oils can help alleviate symptoms of heavy bleeding during periods.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for over bleeding during periods
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Zingiber officinale roots, and 1 cup of carrier oil (such as coconut or olive oil).
Wash the roots clean and chop them into small pieces. Use a cheese grater to grate the roots into smaller pieces.
In a clean glass jar, combine 1 cup of grated roots with 1 cup of carrier oil. Close the jar and let it sit in a dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and keep the liquid.
Store the Zingiber officinale essential oil in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the oil per use, and mix with a carrier oil before applying to the skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains glycyrrhetic acid, a triterpene that has anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce swelling and ease cramping in the uterus, which can contribute to heavy bleeding during periods. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the oil also have astringent properties, which can help to contract the blood vessels and reduce bleeding. The sesquiterpenes in Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils have a calming effect on the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and promoting a more even flow of blood. This combination of properties can help to alleviate heavy bleeding during periods by reducing inflammation, cramping, and promoting a more balanced flow of blood.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for over bleeding during periods
remedy_procedure: Gather 500g of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 2 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil.
Grind the Glycyrrhiza glabra roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 200g of the ground roots with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and place it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the oil extract from the roots.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and using the extracted oil as needed.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help ease heavy periods, but it's not a guarantee it will prevent over bleeding. Some herbal teas, like ginger and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the uterus and reduce cramps. However, the effectiveness of these teas can vary from person to person.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can help with heavy periods, but it's not safe to drink them every day. Some teas, like those with ginger or chamomile, can thin the blood and make bleeding worse. If you're drinking herbal teas, limit them to 1-2 cups per week, and see how your body reacts.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help reduce heavy bleeding during periods, but it depends on the individual. Some people notice a difference within 1-3 menstrual cycles, while others may need to try for 3-6 months. It's essential to drink the teas regularly and be patient, as they work to balance hormones and ease symptoms naturally.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for heavy periods can help ease symptoms. Try sipping it in the morning, about an hour after waking up. This can help calm the body and reduce bleeding. As you go about your day, the tea's soothing effects can continue to work, helping to regulate your flow and reduce discomfort.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for heavy bleeding during periods. Some teas, like ginkgo biloba and saw palmetto, may slow blood clotting. This can increase the risk of bleeding when taking medications like aspirin or heparin. It's essential to check the ingredients of herbal teas and discuss them with your doctor.
faq_pregnant: It's not recommended to drink herbal teas for heavy bleeding during pregnancy, as some herbs can affect the baby's growth or cause other problems. Certain herbs like pennyroyal, blue cohosh, and red clover can be particularly concerning. It's best to stick to plain warm water or other safe options to help manage heavy bleeding.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can help with heavy bleeding during periods, but be cautious while breastfeeding. Some teas can pass into breast milk, which might affect the baby. Peppermint, sage, and pennyroyal teas are known to be problematic. If you're breastfeeding, consider teas like ginger, raspberry leaf, or chamomile, which are generally considered safe.
title_2: 9 Natural Essential Oils for Women with Endometriosis Dealing with Excessive Period Bleeding
